name: TOKEN2049 Singapore
location: Marina Bay Sands Expo & Convention Centre, Singapore
attendance: Record ~25,000 attendees from 160+ countries; sold out. ~500 exhibitors, 300+ speakers. Surrounding TOKEN2049 Week (Sep 29 - Oct 5) featured 500+ side events.
defi_themes: Stablecoins as financial infrastructure / cross-border settlement rails (>$45T in annual stablecoin transaction volume cited; market cap ~$300B, up ~50% in 2025)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization going mainstream (tokenized US Treasuries from BlackRock/Franklin Templeton; tokenized equities and real-world debt instruments)
Institutional DeFi: move from speculative yield farms to institutional-grade infrastructure (custody, audited proof-of-reserves, compliance, regulatory wrappers)
Decentralized perpetual exchanges (perps) scaling and competing on zero/low-fee models (Hyperliquid prominent)
On-chain treasuries / Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) as new standard for on-chain capital deployment and yield optimization
DeFi lending drivers: institutional adoption, RWAs, yield loops, decentralized stablecoins
Sustainable yield via structured products (covered calls, options) rather than mercenary yield farming
Regulation & compliance: MiCA, regulatory clarity, compliant stablecoin design, oracle and liquidity-fragmentation risk
AI + crypto convergence (DeAI) as an adjacent infrastructure theme
Liquidity fragmentation and settlement mismatch cited as the biggest obstacles to scaling tokenization

notable_panels.title: RWAs: Bringing Global Markets On-Chain
notable_panels.topic: Real-world asset tokenization, bringing global markets / treasuries / debt on-chain

notable_panels.title: Curve Finance Stablecoin Summit (TOKEN2049 Week)
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoins, decentralized stablecoin design and DeFi
notable_panels.speakers: Hosted by the Curve Finance team

notable_panels.title: World Liberty Financial (WLFI) session
notable_panels.topic: USD1 stablecoin (described as fully backed by US Treasuries, audited monthly) and DeFi
notable_panels.speakers: Donald Trump Jr., Zach Witkoff (World Liberty Financial)

notable_panels.title: DeFi lending / next wave of DeFi discussion
notable_panels.topic: DeFi lending drivers in 2025: institutional adoption, RWAs, yield loops, decentralized stablecoins; covered calls/options for sustainable yield
notable_panels.speakers: Piers Ridyard (RDX Works / Radix CEO) among speakers

notable_speakers.name: Donald Trump Jr.
notable_speakers.affiliation: World Liberty Financial (Co-Founder) - USD1 stablecoin / DeFi

key_takeaways: Dominant narrative was the shift from speculation to institutional adoption and maturity: DeFi positioned as institutional-grade financial infrastructure, not speculative yield farming.

Stablecoins emerged as the central 'bridge' / Trojan horse asset for crypto into mainstream finance, with massive transaction volume (>$45T cited) and growing market cap; compliant, audited, Treasury-backed designs (e.g., USD1) emphasized.

RWA tokenization moved from experiment to mainstream story, led by tokenized US Treasuries (BlackRock, Franklin Templeton) and tokenized equities/debt; liquidity fragmentation and settlement mismatch remain the key obstacles.
Perpetual DEXs (Hyperliquid and others) are scaling and competing on zero/low-fee models, shifting profitability toward LP mechanisms; on-chain perps gaining institutional attention.
Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) and on-chain treasuries highlighted as a new standard for capital deployment and yield optimization.
Compliance and regulatory readiness (MiCA, audited proof-of-reserves, custody) framed as the differentiator for the next wave of DeFi winners over hype/community metrics.
Asia (and Singapore) reinforced as a leading hub for digital-asset innovation and institutional capital.
Sustainable yield via structured products (covered calls, options) discussed as the path beyond mercenary yield farming.
